{"idea": {"Name": "geometric_quantization_routing", "Title": "Geometric Quantization Routing: Zero-Training Mixed-Precision Inference via PAC-Bayes Stability Bounds", "Short Hypothesis": The local activation geometry of transformer layers strictly bounds their sensitivity to quantization noise. By computing real-time spectral diagnostics (covariance condition number, Fisher discriminant ratio), we can dynamically route layers to lower precision without accuracy degradation, satisfying PAC-Bayes stability constraints while minimizing hidden-state perturbation and enforcing strict wall-clock latency budgets. This setting is optimal because it replaces brittle, calibration-dependent quantization with a theoretically grounded, test-time adaptive pathway that directly couples representation stability to compute allocation, offering a transparent alternative to opaque heuristic profiling.", "Related Work": Existing dynamic quantization methods (e.g., AWQ, Smo
